AboutPage.title: About me
AboutPage.intro1: Hello, my name is Natalia. I'm a frontend developer passionate about creating clean, user-friendly, and responsive web interfaces. I'm working with HTML, CSS, Tailwind CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, Angular, and React. I enjoy building web applications that people find easy to use, and I value being part of projects that have a meaningful impact. My recent experience includes developing web applications for the healthcare sector — helping medical staff, children in intensive care units and families stay connected through intuitive, modern interfaces.
AboutPage.intro2: I find the most inspiration in creating and refining intuitive web interfaces while continually developing my frontend skills. Feel free to check out my projects below!

ProjectsPage.title: Projects
ProjectsPage.demoNote: Demo mode: videostreams are replaced with stock videos

earlyMoves.title: Neolook Early Moves
earlyMoves.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Early Moves application interface
earlyMoves.summary: Early Moves is a software solution to support healthcare professionals and parents in capturing and analyzing infants movements remotely, helping to detect developmental conditions early and provide better care for high-risk or premature babies.
earlyMoves.contribution: I joined the Early Moves project at its start in January 2023, where I was responsible for building the entire frontend while other team members handled the backend. I translated Figma designs into a scalable, responsive Angular application (v15-v18) using Tailwind CSS, and implemented features such as login, video recording, and translation. I also optimized the application for mobile, tablet, desktop, and wide-screen devices and created reliable email templates.

mmcFlyer.title: Máxima MC Information Flyer
mmcFlyer.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Máxima MC information flyer webpage
mmcFlyer.summary: This project is an informational webpage created to help parents prepare before using S2S Family, the software for watching their baby in the NICU. The page provides a clear overview of the software, testimonials from other parents, detailed instructions on installation, and a FAQ section. Its goal is to make it easier for parents to understand and use the system confidently.
mmcFlyer.contribution: I developed the Máxima MC Information Flyer webpage for Neolook and Máxima MC hospital in April 2023. I implemented language auto-detection so the page can be viewed in Dutch or English. To make content management easier for Neolook team members, I used Strapi to store publicly available text for the FAQ and testimonials sections. I ensured the page works well on both mobile and desktop screens and added CSS animations to make the interface more user-friendly.

s2sPlatform.title: Neolook S2S Platform
s2sPlatform.imageAlt: Screenshots of the S2S Platform on desktop, tablet, and phone
s2sPlatform.summary: Neolook's S2S Platform is a system that brings together video and AI solutions for neonatal and pediatric intensive care (NICU and PICU). It offers different modules with specific goals: S2S Family helps parents stay connected with their child, S2S Professional supports nurses and doctors with clinical monitoring and consultation, and S2S Academic provides tools for clinical research.
s2sPlatform.contribution: Over the last three years, I was mainly responsible for developing the frontend of the S2S Platform. My work included implementing new features, pages, and components, ensuring they were responsive and reliable, and making sure the frontend communicated smoothly and effectively with the backend. As the project grew more complex, I also worked on the frontend architecture to make it scalable and maintainable. Among the features I implemented were real-time camera status updates (SSE), grid and list views for camera monitoring (for both nurse stations and care telephones), a web interface for multi-person video calls, feature access and URL protection based on license type (Family, Professional, or Academic), and light/dark modes. I collaborated closely with designers, backend developers, the solution architect, and management, working in sprints with regular demos and sprint planning in Jira.

juniorEinstein.title: Frontend developer
juniorEinstein.description:
Developed UI components and product features for an internal e-commerce platform using React, Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS.
Built reusable components for a shared design system using shadcn/ui and Storybook (Button, Checkbox, Switch, Alert).
Implemented product UI elements such as product cards and filtering interfaces.
Contributed to code reviews and wrote unit tests (Vitest) and end-to-end tests (Cypress) to improve code quality.
Researched internationalization approaches and implemented multilingual support for the application.

neolook.title: Frontend developer
neolook.description:
Led the development of frontend interfaces for healthcare platforms used in neonatal and pediatric intensive care units (NICU/PICU).
Designed and implemented the frontend architecture for several Angular applications, translating Figma designs into scalable, responsive interfaces using TypeScript and Tailwind CSS.
Developed complex UI features including patient and camera monitoring dashboards, data tables, filtering interfaces, and multi-step forms/modals.
Implemented real-time functionality such as camera status updates using Server-Sent Events and video communication using WebRTC.
Built core application infrastructure including routing refactoring, dark/light mode, Dutch-English localization, and API integration (REST, Strapi).
Collaborated closely with backend developers and designers in an Agile environment to deliver reliable healthcare interfaces.
Solutions were deployed in hospitals including Máxima MC (Eindhoven), UMC Utrecht, and UMC Groningen.

abbyy.title: Software Test Engineer
abbyy.description:
Testing of SDK for optical character recognition (Windows, C++).
Full cycle of SDK applications release testing: test plan development and prioritization, test data and test environment preparation, tests implementation, tests results analysis and bug reporting, test automation and regression testing, report writing.
Functional, exploratory and load testing; tests utilities development and support, code review.
Release management, communication with developers and analysts, working in accordance with Agile Methodologies.
